mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 2.0% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 2.0% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payroll employment above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150,000 persons, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening demand-side momentum and forward-looking recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1,400 thousand units for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite HOUST weakness, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below 109.0 (index 2017=100) combined with rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 260) signals stagflationary pressure that compresses equity valuations and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where both INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL > 260, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales (RSAFS) above 530,000 million dollars combined with unemployment rate below 3.7% signals strong consumer demand and tight labor market, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with robust consumer spending
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where both RSAFS > 530,000 and UNRATE < 3.7%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 3.7% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and forward-looking earnings risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early recession
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE > 3.7%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 2.5% combined with month-over-month decline in industrial production signals monetary tightening that suppresses growth expectations and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with weakening supply-side activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 2.5% and INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter remains above 2.0% annualized, signaling sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
